info.title: Go City Trade API
info.description: The Go City Trade API is for trade partners to create and manage their Go City orders. This version of the API is compliant with the [OCTO](https://www.octo.travel/) standards. As of Nov 2024 it is not yet ready for testing or usage but we are providing this documentation so that our API consumers can prepare their migrations. It does not interfere with the V1 API in any way.

tags.name: Authorization
tags.description: We currently support Bearer token authorization on all of the endpoints. You should receive credentials/instructions from your connectivity manager.

tags.name: Octo-Capabilities
tags.description:
Our octo implementation provides certain capabilities when making requests such as including pricing. To do this with us, you may include the **Octo-Capabilities** header, or the **_capabilities** request parameter to enable a capability. Currently only the **octo/pricing** capability is supported.
To enable pricing you can either:
Use the headers - include a header with key **Octo-Capabilities** and value **octo/pricing**.
OR use request parameters- add a request parameter with name **_capabilities** and value **octo/pricing**
In either case, the value is a comma delimited string (ex. 'octo/pricing,octo/content')
Only available in staging as of Aug 12.

tags.name: Products
tags.description: At Go City, our products are our passes. Under the OCTO specification, we have one product per a combination of destination, brand [Go City, Great, Big Bus, Omnia], duration type [Days (All-Inclusive), Choices (Explorer)],  and variant [Standard, Plus, Lite, Deluxe, Single-Sell]. Within each combination, we have multiple options for each duration and type [Pass, Extension], e.g 1-Day Standard Pass or 1-Choice Extension, so within our  Chicago Go City All-Inclusive Standard product, we have 1 Day Standard Pass.  Each option then has three possible units; an Adult unit, a Child unit, and a VIP unit.

tags.name: Availability
tags.description: Even though our products are always available, we have implemented the Availability step of the OCTO journey for the convenience of our API consumers.

tags.name: Bookings
tags.description: To buy a pass you must first create a Booking and then confirm it. With your booking ID you can then make modifications to your pass order.

get.description: This endpoint will fetch the bookings you have made for the given filters. </br> </br> When using this endpoint you must include one of the following query parameters: </br> - `resellerReference` </br> - `supplierReference` </br> - `localDate` </br> - `localDateStart` and `localDateEnd`

parameters.name: resellerReference
parameters.description: The reseller reference on the booking.

parameters.name: supplierReference
parameters.description: The reference provided by the supplier.

parameters.name: localDate
parameters.description: All bookings made for a specific date.

parameters.name: localDateStart
parameters.description: First date of a date range search.

parameters.name: localDateEnd
parameters.description: Last date of a date range search.

parameters.name: productId
parameters.description: The product id to filter by.

parameters.name: optionId
parameters.description: The option id to filter by.

post.description: Create a booking that reserves the availability while you collect payment and contact information from the customer. The booking will remain with status ON_HOLD until the booking is confirmed or the reservation hold expires.

parameters.name: priceDistributionModel
parameters.description: Determines the type of pricing to be used. Possible values are PUBLIC or RESTRICTED. Defaults to PUBLIC if not specified.

post.description: This endpoint confirms the booking so it's ready to be used.

parameters.name: priceDistributionModel
parameters.description: Determines the type of pricing to be used. Possible values are PUBLIC or RESTRICTED. Defaults to PUBLIC if not specified.

post.description: A booking can only be cancelled if `booking.cancellable` is `TRUE`, and it is within the booking cancellation cut-off window.

Check availability before making a booking. This endpoint will return an object for each individual departure time (or day). You have to perform this step to retrieve an `availabilityId` in order to confirm a sale. </br> </br> You must pass in one of the following combinations of parameters for this endpoint: </br> - `localDate` </br> - `localDateStart` and `localDateEnd` </br> - `availabilityIds`

parameters.name: priceDistributionModel
parameters.description: Determines the type of pricing to be used. Possible values are PUBLIC or RESTRICTED. Defaults to PUBLIC if not specified.

get.description: Fetch the status of an existing booking.
Fetch the list of products. Prices are included.

parameters.name: priceDistributionModel
parameters.description: Determines the type of pricing to be used. Possible values are PUBLIC, or RESTRICTED. Defaults to PUBLIC if not specified.

get.description: Fetch the product for the given id. Prices are included.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Product ID – the ID of a unit

parameters.name: priceDistributionModel
parameters.description: Determines the type of pricing to be used. Possible values are PUBLIC, or RESTRICTED. Defaults to PUBLIC if not specified.

id.description: The availability id; needed when booking.

localDateTimeStart.description: The start time for this availability. This will be in the local time zone of the product. An ISO 8601 compliant date and time.

localDateTimeEnd.description: The end time for this availability. This will be in the local time zone of the product. An ISO 8601 compliant date and time.

allDay.description: A boolean field indicating whether this is an all day availability and not a fixed departure time. If this value is true then there will be no other availability object on the same day.

available.description: Whether there is availability for this date / slot.

status.description: The status of that date. Always FREESALE.

vacancies.description: Always null, because status is always FREESALE.

capacity.description: The total capacity on this day.

maxUnits.description: Maximum number of units that can be sold within one booking on this day / slot.

utcCutoffAt.description: The time by which the booking must be confirmed at

unitPricing.description: A list of unit ids with their prices for the availability period. Available only if pricing is requested.

pricing.description: Sum total of the price of all provided units. Available only if pricing is requested and units are provided in the request.

OctoAvailability.description: The availability object that was booked.

original.description: The original price for this product which will be the same or higher than the sale amount. Use this to show a discount has been applied.

retail.description: The sale price that should be charged to customers.

net.description: The wholesale rate Go City will charge for this sale.

currencyPrecision.description: All pricing is given in integers to avoid floating point rounding issues. To convert a price to decimal, divide the price by 10 to the power of currencyPrecision (e.g. for USD, divide the price by 100; for JPY divide by 1).

uuid.description: A UUID you can set when generating the booking to use as an idempotency key.

supplierReference.description: A unique 10-digit order number generated by Go City to identify the booking.

resellerReference.description: A UUID you can set when generating the booking to use as an idempotency key.

testMode.description: If TRUE, booking was created on test mode.

status.description: The status of the booking. `ON_HOLD` - the order has not been confirmed; `CONFIRMED` - order has been placed; `REDEEMED` - passes have been used; `EXPIRED` - the order was not placed successfully; `CANCELLED` - the order has been fully cancelled; `REJECTED` - the order has been rejected

utcCreatedAt.description: An ISO8601 date time in UTC when the booking was created.

utcUpdatedAt.description: An ISO8601 date time in UTC when the booking was updated.

utcExpiresAt.description: An ISO8601 date times in UTC for when this booking is due to expire if the status is ON_HOLD.

redeemedAt.description: An ISO8601 date time in UTC at when the booking was redeemed.

utcConfirmedAt.description: An ISO8601 date time in UTC when the booking was confirmed.

productId.description: The OCTO productId in this booking

product.description: The OCTO product in this booking, including only the options and units booked.

optionId.description: The OCTO optionId in this booking

cancellable.description: A boolean field indicating whether this booking can be cancelled.

cancellation.description: An object with information about why and when the booking was cancelled. This is NULL if the booking is not cancelled.

freesale.description: Whether the booking was made as freesale (without checking availability first).

availabilityId.description: The availability id that was used in the request.

availability.description: The availability object that was booked.

deliveryMethods.description: Delivery methods available for this booking. Always TICKET

voucher.description: How the voucher can be redeemed and whether it already has.

unitItems.description: An array of unit items that will be included in the booking.

pricing.description: The total price of the booking. Only available if pricing is requested.

refund.description: Whether the booking was refunded as part of the cancellation.

reason.description: A text value describing why the cancellation happened.

utcCancelledAt.description: An ISO8601 date time in UTC indicating when the booking was cancelled.

id.description: The id that identifies this option. This is unique within the entire Go City scope. This id may change in certain scenarios due to upcoming development.  Please verify it aligns with the latest implementation if issues arise.

internalName.description: The name Go City calls the option by.

reference.description: An optional code Go City might use to describe the product.

durationValue.description: The duration value of the product - the number of durationType for the product.

availabilityLocalStartTimes.description: This will be an array of all possible start times that can be returned during availability. An all day attraction may have a single value whilst a tour with multiple departure times may have multiple.

cancellationCutoff.description: Human-readable representation of the cancellation cutoff (e.g. -365 days). A negative value is measured **after** the confirmation time.

cancellationCutoffAmount.description: The numeric amount for the cutoff. We provide a negative number when the cutoff is determined by time after purchase, instead of time before travel.

cancellationCutoffUnit.description: Time units used to determine cutoff. Three values are available: hour, minute, day.

requiredContactFields.description: An array of the contact fields required to confirm a booking. These just apply to the lead traveller on the booking and not for every ticket.

restrictions.description: An object containing a fixed list of restrictions for booking the option.

units.description: The list of ticket types (units) available for sale.

pricingFrom.description: Is on the object when pricing capability is requested.

pricing.description: Is on the object when pricing capability is requested.

minUnits.description: The minimum number of tickets that can be purchased in a single booking (null = 0).

maxUnits.description: The maximum number of tickets that can be purchased in a single booking (null = unlimited).

original.description: The original price for this product which will be the same or higher than the sale amount. Use this to show a discount has been applied.

retail.description: The sale price that should be charged to customers.

net.description: The wholesale rate Go City will charge for this sale.

currencyPrecision.description: All pricing is given in integers to avoid floating point rounding issues. To convert a price to decimal, divide the price by 10 to the power of currencyPrecision (e.g. for USD, divide the price by 100; for JPY divide by 1).

OctoPricing.description: A list of the prices this unit can be sold for. There is one item for each currency the unit is available in.

id.description: The id of the product, used for checking for availability and creating bookings for the product. This is unique within the Go City scope. This id may change in certain scenarios due to upcoming development.  Please verify it aligns with the latest implementation if issues arise.

internalName.description: The name Go City calls the product.

locale.description: A valid BCP 47 RFC 5646 RFC 4647 language tag indicating what language this product content is in.

timeZone.description: The IANA TimeZone name this product is located in.

allowFreesale.description: Whether a booking can be made for this product without having to query availability first; always true for Go City.

instantConfirmation.description: Whether bookings will be immediately confirmed when a sale is made; always true for Go City.

instantDelivery.description: This indicates whether the Reseller can expect immediate delivery of the customer's tickets; always true for Go City.

availabilityRequired.description: Whether an availabilityId is required when creating a booking. Without this the booking will be open-dated and not have a specified travel date. Despite a travel date not being required from the customer, this is always true for Go City as if the partner does not supply a travel date they cannot be billed by travel date.

destinationId.description: The Destination ID this product is located in.

destinationName.description: The name of the destination this product is located in.

durationType.description: This is a string which is used to identify the duration type of the product. The duration type is used to determine what kind of product this is.

availabilityType.description: What type of availability this product has: always OPENING_HOURS; Customers can use our passes at any time when the attractions allow.

deliveryFormats.description: An array of formats the API will deliver the tickets as. Possible values are: PDF_URL - A URL to a PDF file which contains all the ticket details;  HTML_URL - A URL to an HTML page containing the ticket details.

deliveryMethods.description: How the formats described in deliveryFormats will be delivered in the booking response, possible values are: TICKET: Individually per unit in the order (i.e. single ticket for each person) VOUCHER: One ticket for the whole booking.

redemptionMethod.description: How the voucher can be redeemed. Possible values are: MANIFEST The guest name will be written down and they just need to show up DIGITAL The tickets/voucher must be scanned but can be on mobile PRINT The tickets/voucher must be printed and presented on arrival.

options.description: An array of all options for this product. All products must have at least one option.

defaultCurrency.description: The default currency of the product. Available only when pricing capability is requested.

availableCurrencies.description: A list of all possible usable currencies for the product. Available only when pricing capability is requested.

pricingPer.description: Indicates that pricing is per UNIT. Available only when pricing capability is requested.

id.description: The id that identifies this unit. This is unique within the entire Go City scope.

internalName.description: A name to help with identifying the unit which is not shown to the customer.
internalName.example: Chicago Go City 1-Day Standard Pass Adult

reference.description: The internal reference used by Go City to identify this unit.

type.description: The base unit type for this unit definition.

pricingFrom.description: A list of the prices this unit can be sold for. There is one item for each currency the unit is available in.

pricing.description: A list of the prices this unit can be sold for. There is one item for each currency the unit is available in.

uuid.description: The id of the unit, this will be unique to the option.

supplierReference.description: A unique 10-digit order number generated by Go City to identify the booking.

resellerReference.description: A UUID you can set when generating the booking to use as an idempotency key.

utcRedeemedAt.description: The ISO8601 date in UTC indicating when the ticket was used at the attraction.

contact.description: Contact details for the guests that will attend the tour/attraction. This is not required, and will not be used.

uuid.description: A unique UUID to identify the booking. Setting this value acts like an idempotency key preventing you from double booking.

productId.description: The product ID for this booking.

optionId.description: The option ID for this booking.

availabilityId.description: The availability ID for the selected timeslot.

expirationMinutes.description: How many minutes to reserve the availability, otherwise defaults to the supplier default amount.

unitItems.description: An list of unit items that will be included in the booking.

priceDistributionModel.description: The price distribution model for this booking.

emailReceipt.description: Whether you want Go City to email the customer their passes.

resellerReference.description: Your reference for this booking. Also known as an Order Number. Must be unique.

contact.description: Contact details for the main guest who will attend the tour/attraction. Contact should be applied to only the booking object. This is required and serves as the associated email on the booking.

unitItems.description: An array of unit items that will be included in the booking. This allows you to provide contact details or a reseller reference for each unit item. Be careful to make sure you include ALL unit items that you also had in the original booking reservation request, if you provide more or less than in the booking reservation call this will change the number of unit items being purchased also

force.description: Whether you want to force cancel this booking

reason.description: A text value describing why the cancellation happened.

localDate.description: A single date for availability check.

localDateStart.description: The start date of the booking.

localDateEnd.description: The end date of the booking.

availabilityIds.description: Filter the results by the given ids.

endpoint.description: The endpoint URL of the supplier's OCTO implementation

OctoSupplier.description: Supplier details following the OCTO specification
